text: This is a video of a laparoscopic central gastrectomy for gastric gist. The case is a forty-one-year-old male who was diagnosed with a large ulcerating mass in the gastric body after a workup for iron deficiency anemia. On examination, his physical exam was unremarkable. A CT and EUS showed an eight centimeter mass in the gastric body.

The surgeon retracts the left lateral segment of the liver using a folding retractor and mobilizes the gastrocolic ligament with a harmonic scalpel, entering the lesser sac. The large gastric tumor is visible bulging through the mid-body of the stomach. Dissection proceeds distally to clear posterior attachments, followed by neurovascular sparing dissection along the lesser curvature with the harmonic scalpel. A window is created from the lesser curve into the lesser sac, facilitating distal transection. The distal margin of the stomach is divided using a linear laparoscopic stapler.

The surgeon completes the dissection of the lesser omentum from the lesser curvature of the stomach. The stomach is rotated counterclockwise, providing clear exposure of the proximal transection margin.

The surgeon removes the specimen through a small extension of the midline incision, displaying the proximal and distal margins of the stomach. A 2-0 silk stay suture is placed to stabilize the gastric remnants. A gastrotomy is created in the proximal and distal parts of the gastric remnants, followed by a stapled anastomosis with two staple fires to ensure a large anastomosis. The gastric defect is closed using a PDS suture.

His post-operative course was unremarkable. He was advanced to a clear diet on post-op day two and was discharged to home on post-op day three on a full liquid. Final pathology showed an eight-centimeter spindle cell neoplasm, which was positive for CD-CAD. CD-CAD was positive for CD-CAD, CD-CAD was positive for CD-CAD, and CD-CAD was positive for CD-CAD. This was all consistent with the gastrointestinal stromal tumor. In summary, laparoscopic central gastrectomy is a feasible operation for gastric neoplasm where radical resection is unwarranted. Thank you.
